summary: Close three actionable M2 audit findings with a real per-hunk external-file merge, arbitrary-length numeric Customizer vectors, and catalog-owned fallback copy, each backed by assertion-level RED and focused GREEN evidence.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

externalInputs.purpose: Confirm the official CodeMirror 6 merge API, unified merge controls, chunk inspection, and read-only editor configuration for external-file reconciliation.

decisions.decision: Use the exact official CodeMirror-family @codemirror/merge 6.12.2 package for external-file comparison and hunk resolution.
decisions.rationale: CodeMirror is already an approved MIT dependency family, and its maintained merge primitives avoid inventing an error-prone diff renderer while preserving the existing editor stack and supply-chain policy.

decisions.decision: Open external modifications side by side by default, offer a unified view with explicit disk/local controls for every chunk, and enable Apply only after every chunk has been resolved.
decisions.rationale: Showing two raw files did not provide an actionable diff. Explicit per-chunk choices prevent an ambiguous mixed state and preserve the command-bus path when the selected result is applied.

decisions.decision: Accept every non-empty numeric vector that the structural parser can validate rather than silently discarding vectors longer than four components.
decisions.rationale: The specification places no four-component ceiling on numeric vectors. Retaining all validated component ranges makes parsing, editing, source writing, and JSON round trips consistent.

decisions.decision: Display stable catalog messages for rejected keybindings and mesh-display failures, while keeping implementation-layer exception text out of user-visible component state.
decisions.rationale: Raw exception messages are not a translation boundary and can change with implementation details. Stable catalog copy satisfies N-4 and keeps the failure state understandable.

testEvidence.test: The focused lifecycle suite requires side-by-side and inline modes, two independently resolved chunks, a disabled Apply control until all choices are made, the exact mixed result, and command-bus application into the active project.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed; Tests 1 failed and 20 passed because the raw preformatted comparison had no Side by side control or hunk actions.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 22 passed. The separated-hunk test produced the exact disk-first/local-second mixed source.

testEvidence.criterion: Numeric vectors are not silently truncated by an undocumented ceiling
testEvidence.test: The parser fixture and focused extraction suite require a five-component stock parameter plus an eight-component source with exact values and component ranges.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed; Tests 1 failed and 3 passed because an eight-component vector was omitted.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 4 passed with every vector component and source range retained.

testEvidence.criterion: User-visible error fallbacks remain inside the message catalog
testEvidence.test: Focused settings and viewer tests require catalogued keybinding and rendered-mesh messages instead of raw implementation exceptions.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 2 failed; Tests 2 failed and 29 passed. The UI exposed the raw keybinding collision and replacement parser error strings.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 2 passed; Tests 31 passed. Both surfaces displayed stable catalog-owned fallbacks.

questions: The command-bus audit note was reviewed against call sites: settings and viewer shared-state mutations already dispatch replace-settings or update-viewer; project export and directory inspection are external effects or local transient UI state rather than bypasses of shared application stores.

Question-blocked preview-cap, final raw-log, measurement, screenshot, camera, tab-close, autosave, and keyboard-picking semantics remain parked exactly where their existing questions require owner direction.
No owner similarity gate, comparison repository, workflow content, or prohibited source was opened or executed.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
